criteria.description: Selects a pure unit boundary and explains why integration or mocks add no detection value.
Derives the inclusive-threshold result of 9000 cents from the contract rather than copying the implementation calculation.
Identifies the existing non-null assertion as shallow evidence.
States or demonstrates that the inclusive-threshold regression test fails against the current implementation.
Keeps additional membership, cap, or boundary cases only when they protect distinct pricing risks.

forbidden.description: Computes expected values by reproducing the production algorithm inside the test.
Escalates the pure pricing rule to an integration or E2E test.
